scope_boundary: Synthetic routing decision lease packet validation only. The harness checks record-level least-capable selection, rejected overprivileged candidates, selected-route authority-envelope containment, readiness fallback, expired-lease residualization, rejected-candidate evidence, residual ownership, and MoECOT source-boundary non-claims. It does not run real routing tasks, train or evaluate a learned router, measure route quality, import MoECOT replay packets, benchmark orchestration, enforce deployed authority, or validate specialist quality.
transition_reason: The routing decision lease harness is useful because it makes route receipts reject overprivileged selection, authority widening, stale leases, missing readiness, missing residual ownership, and source-only MoECOT promotion at record level. It remains synthetic fixture evidence, so this accepted decision blocks promotion for learned-router, route-quality, deployed authority-enforcement, specialist-quality, MoECOT replay, orchestration benchmark, or chapter-core claims.
required_artifacts: real routing task suite with task inputs, expected capability needs, and outcome criteria
learned-router or policy-router implementation with reproducible route logs
route-quality measurement with baselines, negative controls, and residual accounting
imported MoECOT replay packets or archived public replay bundle
independent review before broader support movement
negative_results: missing readiness preserves fallback or residual handling
expired leases residualize rather than select stale routes
source-only MoECOT packets preserve replay gaps and promotion blockers
no chapter core claim is promoted
downgrade_triggers: missing-readiness route selected without fallback or residual handling
MoECOT source-only packet described as replay evidence
harness described as learned-router, route-quality, deployed authority-enforcement, specialist-quality, benchmark, or MoECOT-runtime evidence
support-state promotion without a separate accepted transition
promotion_burden: Move above argument only for a narrowed routing claim after real routing tasks, a learned-router or policy-router implementation, route-quality measurements with baselines and negative controls, imported MoECOT replay or archived public replay fixtures, orchestration benchmark records, deployed authority-enforcement evidence, and independent review are recorded with a separate accepted transition.
reviewer_independence: local maintainer-agent review only; not an independent routing, MoECOT, benchmark, authority-enforcement, specialist-quality, source-interpretation, or external safety audit
non_claims: does not promote Routing Heads and Specialist Cores above argument

does not prove learned-router quality, route-quality dominance, deployed authority enforcement, specialist quality, MoECOT replay, orchestration benchmark performance, runtime behavior, model quality, safety, or ASI capability
does not create an upward support-state transition
does not promote the Routing Heads and Specialist Cores chapter core claim
